title: NIS2 Safety System Integrity Monitoring (SIL / SIS Interlocks)
exclusions: Does not determine legal applicability, complete regulator submissions, approve policy decisions, or replace counsel or competent-authority review. Does not replace onsite safety instrumented function engineering studies or proof-test execution—only visibility of telemetry Splunk receives.
evidence: Scheduled saved search `UC-22.2.40`, resilience dashboard panels, and archived CSV/PDF exports in `index=audit_evidence` showing bypass events joined to permit state.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.21(2)(c) requires business continuity and crisis management. The UC monitors safety-system bypasses and forced interlock states against maintenance permit records. It proves bypass events are logged and correlated with authorised work but cannot confirm that the safety instrumented functions meet SIL requirements, hence partial.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.21(2)(c) (Business continuity and crisis management) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.40: NIS2 Safety System Integrity Monitoring (SIL / SIS Interlocks).
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.40' running on index=sis_audit (HEC), index=itsm (permit-to-work), nis2_sis_critical_tags.csv, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A technician forces a valve interlock on `sis_id=SIS-REACTOR-A` while `snow:sc_task` shows no closed permit linked to that CMDB CI; the search emits at least one stats row with nonzero count and a null or non-Closed permit_state.
controlTest.negativeScenario: During a fully documented outage the same `sis_id` logs bypass strings but the related permit task is `Closed` within the search window, so the permit join excludes the event and the correlation yields no rows.

dataSources: `index=ot_safety` with `sourcetype=sis:status` (interlock state, trip bits, channel healthy flags; fields `sis_id`, `interlock_state`, `quality_flag`, `timestamp_source`). `sourcetype=edge_hub:modbus` from Splunk Edge Hub collectors carrying safety PLC registers (`register_address`, `value_raw`, `unit_id`). `sourcetype=sis:change_log` for configuration/bypass audit lines (`event_type`, `operator_id`, `peer_approved`). Splunk Enterprise Security ([Splunkbase 263](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)) for correlation; Splunk OT Security Add-on ([Splunkbase 5151](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5151)) optional for asset context. Lookup `nis2_sis_inventory.csv`: `sis_id`, `sis_name`, `sil_level`, `last_test_date`, `test_cycle_months`.
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk OT Security Add-on (Splunkbase 5151)
spl:
(index=ot_safety (sourcetype=sis:status OR sourcetype=edge_hub:modbus OR sourcetype=sis:change_log)) earliest=-30d
| eval sis_id=coalesce(sis_id, controller_id, plc_name, unit_id)
| eval event_text=lower(coalesce(event_type, message, Operation, interlock_note, ""))
| lookup nis2_sis_inventory.csv sis_id OUTPUT sis_name, sil_level, last_test_date, test_cycle_months
| where isnotnull(sil_level)
| where match(event_text, "(?i)bypass|force|override|inhibit|maintenance.?mode") OR (sourcetype="sis:status" AND interlock_state IN ("defeated","bypassed","manual_override"))
| join type=left sis_id [
    search index=itsm sourcetype=snow:sc_task short_description="*permit*" earliest=-30d
    | stats latest(state) as permit_state latest(number) as permit_ticket by cmdb_ci
    | rename cmdb_ci as sis_id
]
| where permit_state!="Closed" OR isnull(permit_state)
| eval months_since_test=if(isnotnull(last_test_date), round((now()-last_test_date)/(30*86400),2), null())
| stats count values(event_text) as events max(months_since_test) as months_since_test by sis_id sis_name sil_level permit_state permit_ticket last_test_date test_cycle_months
| sort - count
description: Flags safety-system bypasses, forced interlock states, and configuration events that read as overrides when there is no closed maintenance permit aligned to the same engineering object; inventory fields surface proof-test age for triage but do not alone trigger the alert.
value: Helps the organisation prove that trip paths and safety overrides were either tied to authorised maintenance work or escalated quickly—reducing the chance that an unnoticed bypass becomes the root cause of a major physical incident.
implementation: Land historian and Edge Hub telemetry in `index=ot_safety`. Normalise `sis_id` across `sis:status`, `edge_hub:modbus`, and `sis:change_log`. Maintain `nis2_sis_inventory.csv` with proof-test cadence. Ingest ServiceNow permit tasks into `index=itsm` `sourcetype=snow:sc_task` (Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow, Splunkbase 1928 on HF). Schedule UC-22.2.40 daily with 30-day lookback; page OT/SIS SMEs on new unpermitted bypass clusters.
visualization: Table of sis_id, sis_name, sil_level, events, permit_state; timeline of bypass strings; single value count of open overrides; bar chart by plant if you add `plant_area` to the inventory lookup.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Alerts where Alerts.signature="UC-22.2.40*" by Alerts.signature Alerts.severity Alerts.app | rename Alerts.* as * | sort - count
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Alerts datamodel if ES notables will reuse signatures from this search; otherwise raw-index searches suffice.
knownFalsePositives:
• **Controlled shutdown with word “force” in vendor text** — Distinguish: benign alarm text contains force-load but `interlock_state` stays normal. Suppress: tighten `where` to require `interlock_state IN ("defeated","bypassed")` plus ticket join, or filter the SPL with `where NOT match(event_text,"force.?load")`.
• **PLC download during annual turnaround** — Distinguish: long maintenance window with hundreds of acknowledged changes. Suppress: `nis2_turnaround_windows.csv` time-bound exception lookup keyed on `plant_area` and `_time`.
• **Stale ServiceNow state** — Distinguish: permit closed in the field but `snow:sc_task` still shows Work in Progress. Suppress: reconcile via 15-minute accelerated summary of latest state; manual exception register row with ticket number until ITSM backlog clears.
• **Edge Hub duplicate register polls** — Distinguish: identical Modbus reads every **5 seconds** inflate counts. Suppress: `| bin _time span=1m | stats latest(value_raw) as value_raw by sis_id register_address _time` before bypass logic.
• **Proof-test schedule mismatch** — Distinguish: vendor moved digital twin tests off-cycle. Suppress: update `test_cycle_months` and `last_test_date` columns in `nis2_sis_inventory.csv` after signed SIS verification letter.
equipmentModels: IEC 61511 SIS controllers (Triconex, HIMA, Honeywell Safety Manager, Yokogawa ProSafe-RS)
Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928, change_request for SIS modification tracking)
Splunk Edge Hub (Modbus TCP/RTU and OPC-UA data collection from SIS controllers)
Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud (HEC for safety system telemetry ingest)
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

• **Splunk**: Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud (current). Scheduling requires a search head not under memory pressure (check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=ProcessRunner` for OOM warnings before deploying a new scheduled search).
• **Technology Add-on**: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase app ID 1928) must be installed on search heads and indexers. Verify with `| rest /services/apps/local | search label="Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow" | table label version`.
• **Indexes**: `ot_safety`, `itsm` must exist on all indexers with retention matching the regulation's minimum (PCI: 1y, HIPAA: 6y, NIST 800-53: 1-3y per FIPS 199, SOX: 7y). Set `frozenTimePeriodInSecs` accordingly in `indexes.conf`.
• **Lookups**: `nis2_sis_inventory.csv` must be deployed to the search-tier app and refreshed on a documented schedule. For KV Store lookups, verify with `| rest /servicesNS/-/-/data/transforms/lookups | search name IN ('nis2_sis_inventory.csv')`.
• **Search-head capacity**: Subsearch-based detections need `subsearch_maxtime` ≥ 60s and `subsearch_maxout` ≥ 50000 in `limits.conf`. For high-volume environments, tune `[search] max_searches_per_cpu` up from default.
• **Regulatory mapping**: This detection evidences clause `Art.21(2)(c)`. The control objective is: "Evidence that NIS2 Art.21(2)(c) (Business continuity and crisis management) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.40: NIS2 Safety System Integrity Monitoring (SIL / SIS Interlocks)." — every alert payload should include this text so downstream consumers know the compliance purpose.

## Step 1 — Configure data collection

Data collection is performed by the Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase app ID 1928), landing events in `ot_safety`, `itsm` under `sis:status`, `edge_hub:modbus`, `sis:change_log`, `snow:sc_task`. REST-polled ticket tables produce 500-5000 records per sync per table; at 300s interval expect ~3-8 GB/month for a mid-size service desk.

Configure the input through Settings > Data Inputs > ServiceNow Service Account. A reference `inputs.conf` stanza looks like:

```ini
[snow://{instance_slug}]
url = https://<instance>.service-now.com
table = {table_name}
interval = 300
index = itsm
sourcetype = {sourcetype}
```

The following fields are authoritative for this detection once the TA parses them: `number, state, priority, assignment_group, opened_at, closed_at, sys_updated_on, caller_id, category, subcategory`. Verify they are extracted at search time with `index=ot_safety earliest=-15m | fieldsummary` before scheduling the detection.


One operational consideration shapes the deployment of this detection:


• **Hint 1:** Land historian and Edge Hub telemetry in `index=ot_safety`. Normalise `sis_id` across `sis:status`, `edge_hub:modbus`, and `sis:change_log`. Maintain `nis2_sis_inventory.csv` with proof-test cadence. Ingest ServiceNow permit tasks into `index=itsm` `sourcetype=snow:sc_task` (Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow, Splunkbase 1928 on HF). Schedule UC-22.2.40 daily with 30-day lookback; page OT/SIS SMEs on new unpermitted bypass clusters. The lookup table identified here is a long-lived compliance artifact; version it in source control and track every change with a pull-request review.

## Step 2 — Implement the search

For "NIS2 Safety System Integrity Monitoring (SIL / SIS Interlocks)," the aggregation produces the result shape required by the NIS2 control — each row is a summary record the regulation expects to be presentable at audit time.

This search correlates `index=ot_safety` (sourcetype=`sis:status`) on the outer side with `index=itsm` (sourcetype=`edge_hub:modbus`) on the inner side, joined on `sis_id` across the last 30 days. `type=left` preserves all rows from the outer search; nulls in the inner side indicate missing data that may itself constitute a finding.

Join caveat: Splunk's `| join` subsearch is capped at `max_searches_per_cpu * subsearch_maxout` results (default: 50000). If the inner subsearch exceeds this, rows are silently dropped and the resulting report will under-count. Check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd log_level=WARN subsearch` for truncation warnings. For inner searches expected to return >10000 rows, rewrite as `| stats` with a larger base search and post-filter, or use `| tstats` if a CIM datamodel covers the data.

**CIM / accelerated variant:** The detection also has an accelerated variant using `| tstats` against the CIM `Alerts` datamodel:

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Alerts where Alerts.signature="UC-22.2.40*" by Alerts.signature Alerts.severity Alerts.app | rename Alerts.* as * | sort - count
```

When to prefer this variant: once the underlying datamodel is accelerated (verify in Settings > Data Models > `Alerts` > status=100%), this query runs 10-50x faster than the raw variant and can feasibly cover multi-month windows for audit reporting. When to avoid: if your field extractions are app-specific and the CIM tagging is incomplete, the `tstats` variant will silently under-count. Always compare raw vs accelerated counts on a known day before swapping the production schedule. Maintain both variants in the UC — the raw is the primary for accuracy, the accelerated is the performance optimisation for dashboarding.

**Schedule configuration** (`savedsearches.conf`):
```ini
[UC-22.2.40: NIS2 Safety System Integrity Monitoring (SIL / SIS Interlock]
cron_schedule = 0 3 1 * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -30d
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index
action.summary_index._name = summary_compliance
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.2.40,reg=NIS2
```

## Step 3 — Validate deployment

**(a) Field extraction check:** Run the following to confirm the TA is extracting every field the detection relies on:

```spl
index=ot_safety sourcetype=sis:status earliest=-1h
| fieldsummary
| where count=0
| table field
```

Any row returned is a field the TA claims to extract but isn't appearing in recent data — it's either a rename upstream, a TA version mismatch, or a data-format change.

**(b) Required-field presence:** This detection specifically needs: `sis_id`, `sis_name`, `sil_level`, `event_text`, `permit_state`. Run:

```spl
index=ot_safety sourcetype=sis:status earliest=-1h
| head 100
| table sis_id, sis_name, sil_level, event_text, permit_state
```

Every row should have non-null values for the fields the detection's `where` clause filters on. Nulls cause silent drops in the result set.

**(c) Lookup freshness:** The detection joins against `nis2_sis_inventory.csv`. Check its modification time and row count:

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_sis_inventory.csv
| eval _lookup_row=1
| stats count as rows
```

Compare the row count to the expected baseline; an unexpected drop is a sign the upstream export truncated.

**(e) Negative test:** Generate a synthetic 'should not alert' event using the Splunk Sample Generator or a crafted `| makeresults` event and confirm the detection does NOT fire on it. A detection that fires on everything is equivalent to a detection that fires on nothing.

## Step 4 — Operationalize (dashboard and reporting)

Create a companion dashboard titled `UC-22.2.40: NIS2 evidence` with the following panel layout:

**Panel 1 — Table Of Sis Id (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 2 — Sis Name (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 3 — Sil Level (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 4 — Events (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 5 — Permit State; Timeline Of Bypass Strings; Single Value Count Of Open Overrides; Bar Chart By Plant If You Add `Plant Area` To The Inventory Lookup (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.


Time picker defaults: set to last 30 days with named presets for 24h, 7d, 30d, 90d, and 1y — the auditor needs all five windows without retyping. Enable scheduled PDF export for the full dashboard on the regulation's reporting cadence.

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting and regulation-specific caveats

**No results when violations exist:** The most likely cause is subsearch truncation (see Step 2). Inspect `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=searchprocessor log_level=WARN subsearch` for messages like 'Output result count exceeded 50000.'

**Search times out:** Check the search job inspector (`Activity > Jobs > <this UC's last run> > Inspect`). The phase consuming the time (fetch, command, summary) indicates whether the issue is data-layer (too wide a window, unaccelerated datamodel), command-layer (expensive `transaction` or `eventstats`), or post-aggregation.


**NIS2-specific failure modes:**

• 24/7 reporting obligation (Art. 23): NIS2 requires early warning within 24 hours of awareness of a significant incident. Detections that back this SLA must compute elapsed time from the SOC's first-acknowledged timestamp (not the resolve-time) and must trigger independently of business hours — the regulatory clock does not pause overnight.

• Member-state variation: NIS2 is transposed individually by each EU Member State with some latitude on thresholds and sector scoping. A detection correct for German IT-SiG 3 (BSI) may be misaligned with Dutch CWV or French LPM. Use `nis2_member_state_config.csv` to parametrize thresholds per jurisdiction; never hardcode.

• Essential vs Important entity criteria: the applicable obligations depend on sector + size. Before alerting, verify the monitored entity's designation; a detection firing on an out-of-scope subsidiary causes unnecessary regulatory notifications that erode trust with the national CSIRT.

• Supply-chain (Art. 21(2)(d)) extent: NIS2 explicitly includes direct suppliers' cybersecurity posture. Detections must cover monitored-by-you-not-owned-by-you infrastructure (outsourced SaaS, managed SIEM tenants) — these are in scope even if operationally run by a third party. BA/BAA-style governance applies.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the automatic shutoff gear that keeps plants from hurting people. When someone sidesteps that protection without a proper signed maintenance ticket, or when tests fall overdue, we raise a clear flag so experts can fix it before something breaks for real.
